intent:
Harden MCP Risk Inventory's scanner coverage evidence for common MCP/client configs before the scheduled public signal check. Context: the standing objective is to build and validate an open-source MCP risk inventory CLI/GitHub Action for small engineering teams adopting AI coding tools. The product is public, npm-installable as mcp-risk-inventory@0.1.2, and listed on GitHub Marketplace. Existing public outreach should not be bumped before 2026-07-10T19:30Z. Independent direction review returned GO for a small reversible coverage-hardening pass.

Result wanted: in /company/products/mcp-risk-inventory, add focused fixtures/tests that prove auto-discovery and risk reporting for the most important client-specific config names/locations claimed in the README: Claude Desktop/Claude Code-style files, Cursor project configs, Cline, Windsurf, VS Code MCP JSON, and explicit single-file scans where relevant. Keep changes scoped: improve tests/fixtures and any support-matrix docs only if the verified behavior differs from the current README. Do not cut a release or publish anything externally unless a code/docs fix truly requires it; if release is not cut, record that. Run the existing check/test commands, keep the local checkout clean except intentional changes, and persist a concise product note under /company/product describing what coverage was added, commands run, and whether public package/action versions changed.
accept: Pass if the product checkout contains focused tests/fixtures proving claimed common client config discovery/risk reporting, existing checks/tests pass, and /company/product contains a note documenting coverage added plus release/version status. Fail if outreach channels are bumped, if broad unrelated refactors are made, if tests are not run without a documented blocker, or if README coverage claims remain untested where the goal named them.
